user_query: I’m polishing a small project site to make my interdisciplinary work on consciousness easier to find. Please optimize the on-page SEO of the local HTML pages under site/ using the target keywords in input/keywords.json, and verify all changes using the provided checker. Requirements: 1) Baseline check: - Run the checker to see current issues and create a report: python3 input/seo_check.py --site-dir site --keywords input/keywords.json --out outputs/seo_report.json 2) Edit the existing HTML files in site/ (index.html, phenomenology-intro.html, neural-correlates.html) to meet all of the following for each page, using its primary keyword from input/keywords.json: - contains the page’s primary keyword and has 30–65 characters. - <meta name="description"> contains the page’s primary keyword and has 110–160 characters. - Exactly one <h1> per page, and it contains the primary keyword. - At least one internal link (<a href="...") to a different page in the site directory (i.e., link index.html ↔ phenomenology-intro.html ↔ neural-correlates.html as appropriate). External links do not count. - Every <img> has a non-empty, meaningful alt attribute. - Add a canonical tag in the <head> with <link rel="canonical" href="FILENAME.html"> where FILENAME matches the current file (e.g., href="index.html" for index.html). - Preserve the scientific meaning of the content (accurate to current empirical research), but you may adjust headings, add meta tags, and insert short linking phrases to meet the constraints above. 3) Validation: - Re-run the checker and ensure it passes with exit code 0 and an all-pass report: python3 input/seo_check.py --site-dir site --keywords input/keywords.json --out outputs/seo_report.json --fail-on-issues Deliverables: - Updated files in site/ with the SEO improvements. - The final outputs/seo_report.json indicating overall_pass: true and per-file checks all passing. Do not rename the files or move them. Keep all work within the existing paths and produce the final report at outputs/seo_report.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
